puzzle.title: The Cradle's Testimony
puzzle.intro: In a moment of profound accusation, when silence would have been an admission of guilt, a miraculous voice emerged from an unlikely source. This newborn soul, a sign for all humanity, was given the power of speech to defend the honor of its guardian and proclaim a divine mission. Can you identify this blessed figure whose very first words were a testament to their miraculous origin and purpose?
clues: My arrival in this world was a sign, and I was blessed from the day I was born.
I was given the book and appointed a prophet while still an infant in the cradle.
From clay, I fashioned the shape of a bird, and by Allah's permission, it came to life when I breathed into it.
By Allah's leave, I healed the one born blind and the leper, and I brought the dead back to life.
My followers requested a table spread with food from the heavens, and Allah answered our prayer as a sign for them.
I was not crucified, nor was I killed, but it was made to appear so to them; rather, Allah raised me to Himself.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Yahya
Prophet Ishaq
Prophet 'Isa
Prophet Isma'il
Prophet Musa

trial.label: What was the central accusation I defended against?
options: A plot of betrayal by my own siblings
The rebellion of my people who worshipped a golden calf
An accusation against my mother's chastity
A test of sacrificing my own child
An accusation of sorcery against a ruler
trial.answer: An accusation against my mother's chastity

location.label: Key Element
options: A golden calf
A magnificent palace
A bird
A great ark
A solid iron barrier

outcome.label: Outcome
options: I was raised to Allah and saved from crucifixion
I was swallowed by a great fish and then saved
I lived to a very old age and died peacefully
I was thrown into a fire but remained unharmed
I was martyred by my own people
outcome.answer: I was raised to Allah and saved from crucifixion

puzzle.arabic: وَقَوْلِهِمْ إِنَّا قَتَلْنَا ٱلْمَسِيحَ عِيسَى ٱبْنَ مَرْيَمَ رَسُولَ ٱللَّهِ وَمَا قَتَلُوهُ وَمَا صَلَبُوهُ وَلَـٰكِن شُبِّهَ لَهُمْ ۚ وَإِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱخْتَلَفُوا۟ فِيهِ لَفِى شَكٍّۢ مِّنْهُ ۚ مَا لَهُم بِهِۦ مِنْ عِلْمٍ إِلَّا ٱتِّبَاعَ ٱلظَّنِّ ۚ وَمَا قَتَلُوهُ يَقِينًۢا
puzzle.verse: And [for] their saying, "Indeed, we have killed the Messiah, Jesus the son of Mary, the messenger of Allāh." And they did not kill him, nor did they crucify him; but [another] was made to resemble him to them. And indeed, those who differ over it are in doubt about it. They have no knowledge of it except the following of assumption. And they did not kill him, for certain. (4:157)
